sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Osasuna have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, with a notable 3-1 victory over Rayo Vallecano. Villarreal, however, are in a slump, having lost 4 of their last 5 matches, including a 3-0 defeat to Bayer Leverkusen. This dip in form could impact their confidence going into the game.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Villarreal have dominated Osasuna, winning both matches (2-1 and 4-2). Historically, Villarreal hold an edge with 3 wins and 2 draws in the last 5 matchups, often producing high-scoring games with both teams scoring in the last 5 encounters.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: Osasuna's Ante Budimir (9 goals this season) will be crucial in breaking Villarreal’s defense, while their aerial strength (54.5% duels won) could exploit set pieces. Villarreal's Gerard Moreno (5 goals, standout in head-to-heads) remains a threat despite recent team struggles, with their attacking output still potent at 37 goals this season.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Insight
sections.text: Osasuna, sitting 9th with 25 points, have a solid defensive record (6th least goals conceded), but struggle with possession (14th) and creating big chances (18th). Villarreal, 4th with 41 points, boast a strong attack (37 goals scored), but their recent defensive frailty (9 games without a clean sheet) could be exploited.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Villarreal’s historical dominance over Osasuna and Sofascore’s prediction of a 42% away win probability, they are slight favorites despite their poor recent form. Osasuna’s home advantage at Estadio El Sadar and Villarreal’s defensive issues suggest a competitive match, likely with goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half at Estadio El Sadar between Osasuna and Villarreal was an engaging contest, ending with Osasuna leading 2-1. Both teams showed attacking intent, with Osasuna managing to capitalize on their opportunities more effectively. Villarreal struck first through a penalty, but Osasuna responded quickly with two well-taken headers to take the lead just before the break. The game saw an equal share of possession, indicating a closely fought battle in the midfield.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Osasuna displayed a more clinical edge in front of goal, creating several big chances and converting two of them. Their frequent entries into the penalty area and ability to win possession in the final third suggest a high level of attacking persistence. Villarreal, on the other hand, showed promise with their entries into the final third, but their inability to win possession in dangerous areas limited their threat. Defensively, both teams were evenly matched in duels, though Villarreal edged out slightly in aerial challenges.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players impact
halftimeSections.text: For Osasuna, Ante Budimir and Victor Muñoz stood out with their goal-scoring contributions, showcasing their aerial prowess. Jon Moncayola and Rubén García were instrumental in creating opportunities, providing key assists that turned the game in their favor. On Villarreal’s side, Gerard Moreno was the standout performer, converting a penalty with composure and posing a consistent threat. Dani Parejo also played a crucial role in midfield, attempting to orchestrate attacks with precise passing and strong defensive work.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Osasuna dominate the early momentum, with consistent attacking spells pushing Villarreal onto the back foot. There were moments where Villarreal fought back with notable shifts in momentum, particularly around the time of their penalty goal, briefly taking control. However, Osasuna regained their attacking rhythm with sustained pressure leading to their equalizer and eventual lead. Towards the end of the half, momentum swung back in Osasuna’s favor, culminating in a strong push just before the whistle.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Osasuna will likely aim to maintain their aggressive approach in the final third, using their home advantage to press for more goals. Villarreal, trailing by one, will need to improve their conversion rate and capitalize on their entries into dangerous areas. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, with Villarreal possibly introducing fresh legs to regain control in midfield. Discipline will be key for both sides, as each team has already picked up a yellow card, and avoiding further bookings will be crucial to maintaining their full complement of players.
